74778.subject: Seismic by-election
74778.body:
Editor's Digest 

ąÉą▓čéąŠčĆ: Financial Times


ąÉą▓čéąŠčĆ: Financial Times

    
    
    ą¤čĆąŠčćąĖčéą░ą╣čéąĄ čåčÄ čüčéą░čéčéčÄ ąĮą░ LinkedIn, čēąŠą▒ ą┐čĆąĖčöą┤ąĮą░čéąĖčüčÅ ą┤ąŠ ąŠą▒ą│ąŠą▓ąŠčĆąĄąĮąĮčÅ
ą¤čĆąŠčćąĖčéą░čéąĖ ąĮą░ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/seismic-by-election-financial-times-j7uge?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BgRFWzET6SeC5M6AOITYCUQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1mCgMunOx98Yk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqjvnmdw~qg-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqjvnmdw-qg
  
Hello from the FT newsroom, where we are gearing up for what could be a seismic few days in British politics. If, as many pollsters believe, Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham wins the Makerfield by-election on Thursday, he will be hot favourite to replace Sir Keir Starmer as UK prime minister. The momentum for a Burnham leadership will accelerate and Starmer could be out of a job in weeks. So what would a Burnham government mean for British taxpayers? HereŌĆÖs what we know so far. In other news, the US and Iran have agreed a 60-day extension of their truce. The text of the deal, which was signed on Wednesday, confirms the details that the FT has been reporting (you can read them here). But critics have been quick to point out its similarity to former US president Barack ObamaŌĆÖs 2015 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, which President Donald Trump ditched in his first term. As our chief foreign affairs commentator Gideon Rachman argues, TrumpŌĆÖs deal after four months of war reflects the failure of the military option in Iran. And ŌĆ£it is easy to see how it could all break downŌĆØ. My choices this week When the US government moved in 2019 to cut Huawei off from American technology, most analysts saw it as a death sentence for the Chinese tech giant. They were wrong. Here is the inside story of HuaweiŌĆÖs comeback. EuropeŌĆÖs top tankmaker KNDS could soon file for an initial public offering at a valuation of up to Ōé¼20bn. Find out why a vet, a stairlift salesman and a Mozart scholar are in line for billions when KNDS lists. (Free to read) Revolut now claims a total of 75mn customers. But it is in Ireland that the UK fintech has had the greatest success in seizing market share from rivals old and new. FT columnist Patrick Jenkins explains why. M&S clothing was once known for its mid-life mum basics, not trends. But as our fashion editor Elizabeth Paton writes, BritainŌĆÖs favourite retailer is undergoing a radical overhaul to become a go-to fashion destination. When the Colombian government signed a peace deal with leftwing rebels in 2016, many hoped a new era of stability would begin. But since then, cocaine production in the country has tripled. Who is behind the new drugs boom? England going out is a beloved ritual of World Cups for people all around the world. But this year, connoisseurs of the spectacle might be disappointed. Our sports columnist Simon Kuper

74777.subject: ­¤Ś×’ĖÅ $59K to $66K in One Week: The Iran Deal, SpaceX and What Warsh Does Next
74777.body:
Three macro events in one week. Here is what each one meant for
crypto.    


Kraken ( " target= )  -->  Kraken ( " target= )



**********************************
Compass
**********************************

Delivering Insights & News

See latest prices (
https://link.kraken.com/f/a/Q87zKQOHevB1T9NQ8GBYOg~~/AAQRxRA~/z7L01YXc0UsFX1uJ5VCCs1E_bKe6oyPAQ0h6DtL8XsiVHJ3YcZzppY9Suwu57Wx4JzrypiQLslT3L75F3qO6Ex6PkuK3II1xmUfCgZ4iQyMAWU5J0QI5FARurERKB3fbqHvQQ9RpLUXeCqo_ZIFDfg~~
)     Kraken              

*****************************************************************
Iran Deal, CPI at 4.2% and a SpaceX Debut: Bitcoin Recovers to
$66K Ahead of the FOMC
*****************************************************************

June 12 ŌĆō 18, 2026

Bitcoin moved from a weekly open near $62,000 to a high of
$66,483 by Sunday after a US-Iran peace framework, announced June
14 and due to be signed in Switzerland on June 19, triggered a
broad risk-on shift across global markets. The range represents a
gain of approximately 7 percent across the week, the assetŌĆÖs
strongest seven-day performance since April.

The week was not without friction: May CPI printed at 4.2
percent year-over-year on June 10, matching consensus but
confirming the third consecutive month of acceleration, and the
SpaceX Nasdaq debut on June 12 absorbed $75 billion in capital
from a market that was already stretched. Fear and Greed has
recovered from a reading of eight last week to 29 at the time of
writing. With the FOMC meeting now beginning today, and Chair
WarshŌĆÖs first press conference expected to carry particular
weight, the question heading into the weekend is whether the
geopolitical relief that drove this recovery can hold against a
macro backdrop that has not structurally shifted.





*****************************************************************
Market
Overview
*****************************************************************

An Accord in the Gulf Drove CryptoŌĆÖs Best Week Since April. The
FOMC Will Test Whether It Holds. (

74776.subject: Crypto Biz: Is AI the exit strategy for miners?
74776.body:
Crypto Biz Newsletter

ąÉą▓čéąŠčĆ: Cointelegraph


ąÉą▓čéąŠčĆ: Cointelegraph

    
    
    ą¤čĆąŠčćąĖčéą░ą╣čéąĄ čåčÄ čüčéą░čéčéčÄ ąĮą░ LinkedIn, čēąŠą▒ ą┐čĆąĖčöą┤ąĮą░čéąĖčüčÅ ą┤ąŠ ąŠą▒ą│ąŠą▓ąŠčĆąĄąĮąĮčÅ
ą¤čĆąŠčćąĖčéą░čéąĖ ąĮą░ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/crypto-biz-ai-exit-strategy-miners-cointelegraph-2jr2c?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BGRPKsjl0QiaoTlhWXoIofw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3n9yYE1bJ38Yk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqjvq700~qj-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqjvq700-qj
  
For years, Bitcoin miners were little more than leveraged bets on BTC prices. ThatŌĆÖs changing fast. As mining margins tighten and demand for AI computing accelerates, the industryŌĆÖs biggest players are discovering that access to power and data center infrastructure may be more valuable than hash rate. That shift gained further validation this week as Nvidia reportedly prepared a $20 billion bond sale to finance the next phase of its AI expansion, reinforcing the multi-year investment cycle that Bitcoin miners are increasingly positioning themselves to serve. Elsewhere, the tokenized real-world asset market continued to defy the broader crypto downturn, Ripple expanded its African payments push with an investment in Flutterwave and former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried failed to overturn his fraud conviction. NvidiaŌĆÖs $20 billion bond offering reinforces Bitcoin miner AI pivot Nvidia wants to sell $20 billion-worth of bonds to finance the next phase of its AI expansion, reinforcing the growth trend that has prompted Bitcoin miners to pivot toward AI and data center infrastructure. Bloomberg reported Monday that the chipmaker is pursuing a multi-part bond offering to fund AI-related investments and refinance existing debt. The longest-dated bonds are expected to offer considerably higher yields than comparable US Treasury securities. The sustained AI buildout has created new opportunities for Bitcoin miners, many of which are repurposing their energy-intensive facilities and power infrastructure for high-performance computing and AI hosting as mining economics remain under pressure. Companies including HIVE Digital, Hut 8, CleanSpark and TeraWulf are increasingly positioning themselves as AI infrastructure providers. Source: Cointelegraph Continue ReadingŌĆ” Tokenized RWAs defy crypto bear market The tokenized real-world asset (RWA) market continues to grow despite broader crypto weakness, with the total value of onchain financial assets surpassing $43 billion ŌĆö a 37% increase over the past six months, according to Token Terminal. Tokenized funds make up the overwhelming majority of the RWA market, representing nearly 80% of all onchain financial assets, though commodities and tokenized stocks are gaining traction. The sectorŌĆÖs momentum comes as major financial institutions forecast significant long-term growth. Standard Chartered expects tokeniza

74775.body:

 The Economist

The Economist This Week

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MTF9AAcAAAAAAuDmXdlOqfSrq6UCRYwOCHFbyAb-RcN7yChU5Aw67pNYE4DWbOoANOVHsbq4HcYUEfNbkUTwIrFay4FbdOcuPrlvjO1eG5mXnq8rp2Wq8A 



https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MTF9AAcAAAAAAuDmXdlPgnjCwuKSLsbkwI4UYJJZYHAWYFFpeWdqfO1GAbTWVtk4KT0naCnSldCMQmPdr2AULjkQ0R2fbmUCVsaI41LxdBTvCCbS 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MTF9AAcAAAAAAuDmXdlQz-glmKTmZ_vvLu4DNVOa6xFpGvS6uq16dGAf-XR0i-JFh-vo3UifCm7jv8cG-SevDqAcGDGeNcJwSYR1zphYnEeSDcnE 



Robert Guest

Deputy editor



 My former neighbour, a delightful, fiercely independent lady who died in her mid-nineties, often told me she hated computers. She didn’t own one, didn’t want one and bristled when her bank suggested she switch to an online account on the smartphone she didn’t have. It would have been pointless to argue that banking apps are actually quite convenient. She didn’t want to change.


 Both our cover stories thi
